More recently the company has added AI-assisted capabilities to speed up analysis, such as automated transcription, summarization, and pattern identification across multiple sessions, reflecting the same industry-wide shift toward AI-augmented qualitative research tooling seen across the UX research category. Lookback also integrates with participant recruitment services, allowing teams to source research participants without leaving the platform, and offers guest access so that stakeholders across an organization, not just dedicated researchers, can observe sessions live or review recordings later. Lookback's pricing is structured as four annual plans that scale by the number of research sessions and collaborators included. The Freelance plan costs 299 dollars per year and includes 10 sessions, one included participant-recruitment fee through Lookback's panel, a single seat (with unlimited guest viewers), all core platform features, and chat support, making it suited to independent researchers or consultants running occasional studies. The Team plan costs 1,782 dollars per year and includes 100 sessions, 10 included panel recruitment fees, 10 collaborator seats with unlimited guests, the same full feature set, plus a Security Self-Assess option and chat support, aimed at small in-house research teams. The Insights Hub plan costs 4,122 dollars per year and steps up to 300 sessions, 30 included panel recruitment fees, 30 collaborator seats, a more thorough Security Assessment Assist option, and priority chat support, intended for larger organizations running research across multiple product teams. An Enterprise plan above that offers unlimited sessions, custom participant allocation and seat configuration, a full security review, custom legal terms, single sign-on, invoicing or purchase-order billing, team training, audits, and dedicated customer success management, aimed at large enterprises with procurement and compliance requirements. Customers who exceed their included session allotment can purchase additional 10-session packages, priced at 299 dollars for Freelance, 178 dollars for Team, and 137 dollars for Insights Hub, showing per-session cost declining at higher tiers. Participants sourced through Lookback's own panel outside of included allotments cost 49 dollars each on a pay-as-you-go basis, with discounted rates available for bulk purchases of 10 or more, and Lookback charges a 6 percent incentive management fee when participant incentives are distributed through the platform itself. The primary audience for Lookback is UX researchers, product designers, and product managers at software and digital product companies who need to run qualitative research, from early concept testing through usability validation of shipped features, particularly teams that want facial and vocal reaction data alongside screen behavior rather than screen recordings alone. Its main competitors include UserTesting, Maze, dscout, UserZoom (now part of UserTesting), and Userlytics, all of which compete in the broader user research and usability testing category with varying emphases on moderated versus unmoderated testing, AI-assisted analysis, and participant panel access. Lookback differentiates itself through its long-standing focus on simultaneous face, voice, and screen capture for genuinely qualitative insight, its flexibility across both live moderated and asynchronous SelfTest formats within a single platform, and its comparatively lean, session-based pricing model that lets smaller teams and independent researchers access enterprise-grade research tooling without committing to the largest platform tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Lookback was founded in 2013 by Henrik Mattsson as a remote-first, globally distributed company, built around the idea of capturing a research participant's face, voice, and screen simultaneously for richer qualitative insight than screen recording alone.

The company remains relatively lean, with roughly a few dozen employees spread across North America, Europe, and Oceania, and offers both live moderated interviews and asynchronous unmoderated testing through its SelfTest feature.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Lookback supports live moderated sessions with silent observers, unmoderated SelfTest studies, session highlighting for tagging key moments, and AI-assisted transcription and summarization across sessions.
The platform integrates with participant recruitment panels and supports guest access so stakeholders across an organization can watch or review research without a dedicated researcher seat.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Lookback offers four annual plans, Freelance at 299 dollars per year, Team at 1,782 dollars per year, Insights Hub at 4,122 dollars per year, and custom-priced Enterprise, scaling by included sessions, collaborator seats, and participant recruitment fees.
Additional session packages, pay-as-you-go participants at 49 dollars each, and a 6 percent incentive management fee apply on top of the base annual plans.

features.title: Simultaneous face, voice, and screen capture
features.description: Records participant reactions alongside on-screen behavior for richer qualitative research signal.

features.title: Live moderated sessions
features.description: Researchers guide participants in real time over video, with silent observers able to watch and take notes.

features.title: SelfTest unmoderated testing
features.description: Participants complete tasks and questions independently and asynchronously, with automatic screen, audio, and camera recording.

features.title: Session highlighting
features.description: Timestamp and tag key moments during or after a session to quickly build shareable highlight reels.

features.title: AI-assisted analysis
features.description: Automated transcription, summarization, and pattern identification across multiple research sessions.

features.title: Participant recruitment integration
features.description: Source research participants through Lookback's own panel without leaving the platform.

features.title: Guest access
features.description: Stakeholders outside the research team can observe live sessions or review recordings via guest links.

features.title: Enterprise security and compliance options
features.description: Security review, custom legal terms, and SSO available on higher tiers for enterprise procurement needs.

pros: Captures face and voice reactions alongside screen behavior, not just screen recordings
Supports both live moderated and asynchronous unmoderated research in one platform
Session-based annual pricing lets independent researchers access the tool for a relatively low entry cost
Guest access broadens research visibility to non-researcher stakeholders
Built-in participant recruitment panel simplifies sourcing research subjects
cons: Session-based pricing plus per-participant and incentive management fees can add up for high-volume research programs
Entry Freelance plan is limited to a single seat, restricting collaboration
Company is relatively small compared to larger competitors like UserTesting
Enterprise-grade security and compliance features are reserved for the top two tiers
Pay-as-you-go participant sourcing at 49 dollars each is priced at a premium versus buying in bulk

faqs.question: What is Lookback used for
faqs.answer: Lookback is a user research platform for running live moderated interviews and unmoderated usability tests, capturing participant screen, face, and voice simultaneously for qualitative product research.

faqs.question: How much does Lookback cost
faqs.answer: Lookback's annual plans start at 299 dollars per year for the Freelance tier with 10 sessions, scaling up to 1,782 dollars for Team, 4,122 dollars for Insights Hub, and custom pricing for Enterprise.

faqs.question: Who founded Lookback
faqs.answer: Lookback was founded in 2013 by Henrik Mattsson and has operated as a remote, globally distributed team since its founding.

faqs.question: What is SelfTest in Lookback
faqs.answer: SelfTest is Lookback's unmoderated testing feature that lets participants complete research tasks independently and asynchronously, with the platform automatically recording their screen, audio, and camera.

faqs.question: Does Lookback help with participant recruitment
faqs.answer: Yes, Lookback integrates with its own participant panel so researchers can recruit study participants directly within the platform, either included in a plan or on a pay-as-you-go basis.

faqs.question: How is Lookback different from UserTesting or Maze
faqs.answer: Lookback emphasizes simultaneous face, voice, and screen capture for both live moderated and unmoderated research in one platform, while competitors like UserTesting and Maze have different emphases on panel scale, quantitative testing, and AI-assisted analysis.

use_cases.title: Moderated usability interviews
use_cases.description: UX researchers run live video sessions with participants, guiding them through tasks while observing facial reactions and verbal feedback in real time.

use_cases.title: Unmoderated concept and usability testing
use_cases.description: Teams deploy SelfTest studies to a broader participant pool to validate a design or concept asynchronously without scheduling live sessions.

use_cases.title: Stakeholder research visibility
use_cases.description: Non-researcher stakeholders use guest access to watch live sessions or review highlight reels, building shared understanding of user pain points across the organization.

features: Custom participant allocation and seat configuration
Team training, audits, and CSM support
